plang.note: Deterministic across 2 consecutive clean-binary runs; git status clean after each (zero .pr rewritten). The v8 reproducibility blocker is resolved.

coverage.note: coder v13 is test-only (two new C# files). Both honest; set-path MaterializeFailed contract independently mutation-verified by tester.

findings.issue: Carry-forward of v8 F2 (now non-blocking, fixtures stable). No end-to-end PLang goal walks set -> store -> retrieve -> load -> throw for a LAZY strict image. SetAsImageGifStrictMismatch sets 'photo.png' as image/gif strict but only asserts Type.Name/Kind; it never forces a byte load, so the deferred StrictKindMismatchException never fires in PLang. The throw-at-load is unit-tested directly on image (LazyPathHandleTests.BytesAsync_StrictKindMismatch) and the set-seam imprint survival is established by C# (Cut2). The PLang goal layer does not exercise the seam end-to-end.
findings.impact: If a future refactor drops the strict imprint at the variable.set seam for path-backed values, no PLang goal test would catch it — only the C# unit tests would. Low risk: the C# layer is covered and deterministic.
findings.suggestion: Add a goal that reads %img.Width% or %img.Bytes% after a mismatched strict set on the committed photo.png fixture and asserts the error surfaces (test.report error-capture). Optional polish — C# coverage already pins the contract.

summary: PASS. The branch I FAILed at v8 (PLang suite not reproducibly green; committed .pr stale vs stage-4 variable.set.Type entity; 0-4 flapping fails, '262/262' a warm-cache artifact) is honestly green after the lazy-deserialize merge. Clean-binary PLang 273/273/0/0-stale, IDENTICAL across two consecutive runs, git status clean after each (zero .pr rewritten) — the reproducibility blocker is dead. C# 4025/4025/0 on clean rebuild. coder v13 is test-only: MaterialiseErrorPathTests (+2 set-path) and SignedDataSurvivesVariableSetListTests — both honest (assert Error.Key=='MaterializeFailed' distinct from NotFound; signature survives the real variable.set handler and verifies). I independently mutation-tested the set-path MaterializeFailed contract: neutralizing the guard at variable/list/this.cs:311 flips exactly those two tests red, passes on revert; source reverted clean. Strict-kind enforcement coverage (read-lift Cut2 + lazy path-backed LazyPathHandleTests) survived the merge intact. One minor carry-forward (no end-to-end PLang goal for lazy strict image load-throw), non-blocking.
